Agentic AI Implementation Brief
Regional Amplifier Channels for the Tri-County Marketing & Business Support 
Landing Site
Prepared for implementation on the current Netlify landing site. Version date: 2026-06-13.
Purpose
This brief gives step-by-step instructions for an agentic AI to incorporate verified mailing-list, newsletter, events-calendar, 
business-directory, visitor-guide, and regional promotion-channel research into the current landing site. The brief is written to 
reduce inferential inaccuracies, false claims, and unsupported promotion promises.
Terminology
The page may be called a landing site if it functions as the entry point for a broader guide, resource directory, templates, forms, 
and task workflows. If it remains a single static page, landing page is more precise. Public-facing recommendation: Tri-County 
Regional Resource Landing Site or Tri-County Marketing & Business Support Landing Site.
Non-Negotiable Constraint
Do not infer advertising availability, submission acceptance, costs, deadlines, list ownership, 
audience size, or endorsement.
Only classify a channel by evidence that is visible in the source.
If a source shows a newsletter signup, say newsletter/signup exists.
If a source shows a calendar, say calendar exists.
If a source shows a submit-event form, say submit-event pathway exists.
If a source does not explicitly say it accepts outside advertisements, mark paid ad status as Unknown 
- verify.
1. Required Site Placement
Add a new primary section to the landing site titled:
Regional Amplifier Channels: Newsletters, Calendars, Directories, and Visitor Guides
Place this section after the Start Here decision flow and before county-specific campaign recipes. This placement lets users first 
identify their goal, then immediately find channels where their event, listing, or announcement may travel.
Recommended Navigation Order
Home
Start Here
Regional Amplifier Channels
Promote an Event
Media & Calendar Contacts
Business Directories
Flyer / Offline Outreach
County Campaign Recipes
Templates
Metrics & Reporting
Submit / Correct Information
About / Methodology
If the landing site remains one page
# Regional Amplifier Channels
# Event Calendars
# Newsletters & Mailing Lists
# Business Directories
# Tourism Promotion
# Anchor Venue Lineups
# Ask About Advertising or Placement
# Submission Templates
2. Public-Facing Intro Copy
Regional Amplifier Channels are newsletters, events calendars, tourism websites, business directories, 
venue lineups, municipal update systems, and visitor-guide channels that can help a business, 
nonprofit, artist, or event organizer reach more people.
Some channels accept public event submissions. Some maintain business directories. Some offer 
newsletter signups, visitor-guide pathways, social media co-promotion, or paid advertising. 
Availability varies by organization, so each listing should be checked before use.
Use this section to identify where to submit your event, request a listing, ask about newsletter 
placement, or verify paid and free promotional opportunities.
3. Classification Rules
Tag Use only when evidence shows Do not claim
Verified calendar A calendar, event page, or lineup exists. That outside events are accepted unless a 
submission form/rule exists.
Verified newsletter/signup The source uses subscribe, newsletter, sign up, 
mailing list, or updates language.
That third-party ads/submissions are accepted.
Verified directory A business/member/listing directory exists. That any business can be listed unless an add-
listing form/rule exists.
Verified submission form A form or explicit instruction accepts 
event/listing/marketing submissions.
That submission guarantees publication.
Promotion possible - verify The channel has relevant audience reach but rules 
are unclear.
Any availability, pricing, or deadline.
Paid ad status unknown No explicit ad-rate or paid-placement evidence is That advertising is available.
present.
Needs contact confirmation Source exists but exact pathway is missing or stale. Operational details.
4. Required Channel Table Format
Create a visible table on the landing site using this schema. Do not remove the Source URL or Verification columns; they are the 
anti-hallucination layer.
Column Required content Allowed values / notes
Channel Name of organization, venue, tourism site, city 
page, or business.
Use exact public-facing name from source.
Area served Town/county/region. Use town + county where possible.
Channel type Newsletter, event calendar, directory, visitor guide, 
venue lineup, social media co-op, etc.
Multiple values allowed.
What users can ask/submit Plain-language action. Example: Submit event, request listing, ask about 
ads, request calendar placement.
Best for Use-case guidance. Events, tourism-facing business, arts, lodging, 
nonprofit program, etc.
Paid/free status Known status or uncertainty. Use Unknown - verify unless source explicitly 
states.
Verification status Evidence tag. Use only approved status tags.
Source URL Direct source URL. Plain URL.
Implementation note Caveat or instruction. Keep concise.
5. Step-by-Step Implementation Instructions for Agent
Step 1 - Create the section shell
Add a page or anchor section named Regional Amplifier Channels. Place it after Start Here and before County Campaign 
Recipes. Add short intro copy and a warning that advertising availability must be verified.
Step 2 - Add filter/category cards
Create cards or tabs for Event Calendars, Newsletters & Mailing Lists, Business Directories, Tourism/Visitor Guides, Anchor 
Venue Lineups, and Ask About Advertising or Placement.
Step 3 - Add the master channel table
Use the required table schema. Populate only with verified rows in this brief. Do not add unsourced rows.
Step 4 - Add evidence tags
Each row must carry at least one evidence tag: Verified calendar, Verified newsletter/signup, Verified directory, Verified 
submission form, Promotion possible - verify, Paid ad status unknown, or Needs contact confirmation.
Step 5 - Add action language
For each row, phrase the action as ask/submit/request/verify. Avoid promise language such as advertise here, get featured, or 
guaranteed placement.
Step 6 - Add the promotion packet checklist
Insert the Before You Contact a Channel checklist below the table. This makes the section operational, not just informational.
Step 7 - Add the Ask About Placement template
Place the outreach email template below the checklist. Make it copy-paste ready.
Step 8 - Add a verification warning
Display a callout near the table: Paid advertising, free placement, editorial inclusion, deadlines, and approval rules must be 
verified directly with each organization.
Step 9 - Connect to templates
Link each use case to existing or new templates: calendar listing, event blurb, radio PSA, social caption, press release, directory 
listing request, and sponsor/funder recap.
Step 10 - Add QA pass
Check that no row says accepts ads, free ad, paid placement, newsletter submission, or guaranteed calendar listing unless the 
source explicitly says that. Otherwise use ask about or verify directly.
6. Before Contacting a Channel: Promotion Packet
Prepare a small promotion packet before contacting any calendar, newsletter, directory, visitor guide, 
or tourism partner.
Include:
- Event or business name
- Date, time, and location, if event-based
- One-sentence description
- 50-word description
- 100-word description
- Website, ticket link, or contact link
- Contact name, email, and phone
- Square image
- Vertical image
- Printable flyer, if available
- Accessibility notes
- Whether the item is free, ticketed, nonprofit, youth-focused, tourism-facing, or business-related
Do not send only a flyer image. Calendars and newsletters usually need plain text they can copy, 
searchable details, and a direct contact.
7. Submission Etiquette / Anti-Spam Note
Use these channels respectfully. Submit only relevant events, listings, or announcements. Check each 
organization's rules before sending materials. Do not assume a newsletter, calendar, or directory 
accepts paid ads, free listings, or outside submissions unless the source says so directly. When in 
doubt, ask what formats they accept.
8. Verified Channel Data to Implement
Populate the landing site table with the rows below. Keep source URLs visible or attached to each row. If space is limited on mobile, use expandable cards that preserve the same 
fields.
Channel Area Types Status Use Source URL
Red River Brewing Company & Distillery Red River / Colfax Own-site newsletter / mailing list Verified newsletter/signup Direct proof of a business-originating 
mailing list. Add to directory row as direct 
mailing-list evidence.
https://www.redriverbrewing.com/

newsletter signup
Verified calendar + verified 
newsletter/signup
Anchor venue/resort event lineup and 
business-originating newsletter channel.
https://www.angelfireresort.com/events/

ecosystem. Verify whether it is same 
operator/contact list before merging.
https://

plan-your-trip/summer-events/

newsletter; vacation guide
Verified calendar + submit event + 
newsletter/signup
High-priority regional amplifier. Do not 
classify as individual business list unless 
tied to a business profile.
https://redriver.org/events/

pathway; newsletter
Verified visitor-guide channel Use for visitor-facing businesses and 
events; label as tourism/visitor guide, not 
guaranteed ad inventory.

vacation-guide/

newsletter signup

businesses, lodging/property listings, and 
public events.
https://visitangelfirenm.com/get-listed/

newsletter

options

subscription evidence.

request

channel

amplifier. Include review/eligibility caveat.
https://visittrinidadcolorado.com/
resources-for-locals/

process
Verified reviewed submission pathway Use to prevent overclaiming. Pair with 
note that acceptance is not automatic.
https://visittrinidadcolorado.com/
resources-for-locals/submit-your-event/

business news/resources/events

signup pathway needs confirmation
Add as municipal newsletter lead; mark 
signup/submission process as Needs 
verification until form/contact is 
confirmed.

economicdevelopment_newsletter.php

events

found

channel, not a list-serv unless 
subscription evidence is found later.
https://www.mainstreetlive.org/

promotion target for arts/performance 
work.
https://www.mainstreetlive.org/2026-
calendar

Veta / Cuchara

Needs verification label.
https://huerfano.us/

events; subscribe to updates

assume outside advertising or open 
event-submission unless confirmed.
https://townoflaveta-co.gov/

list; municipal calendar navigation

category

verified

source; search separately for 
subscribe/sign-up before classifying as 
mailing list.
https://www.lavetacreativedistrict.org/
festivals-and-signature-events
9. Best-Use Matrix
User is trying to promote Best channel categories Implementation note
Public event Event calendars; tourism calendars; venue lineups; 
city/community calendars
Use calendar listing template and event image 
package.
Visitor-facing business Tourism directories; visitor guides; chamber/tourism 
sites; Google Business Profile
Use directory listing request template.
Restaurant/retail special Newsletter opportunities; social media co-op; paid 
ad inquiry; local flyers
Do not imply newsletters accept ads unless 
confirmed.
Art show/performance Venue lineups; arts calendars; tourism calendars; 
local media
Use event pitch plus square/vertical images.
Nonprofit program City calendars; newsletters; radio PSA; partner 
organizations
Use community announcement language, not 
commercial ad language.
Grant-funded project Media coverage; newsletter mentions; partner 
reposts; post-event reporting
Track reach and screenshots for reports.
Seasonal tourism campaign Tourism sites; visitor guides; lodging partners; 
regional newsletters
Ask about deadlines, eligibility, and paid/free status.
10. Copy-Paste Outreach Template
Subject: Question About Promotion / Advertising Opportunities
Hello,
I am reaching out to ask whether [organization/publication/site name] accepts event listings, 
newsletter submissions, business directory updates, paid advertisements, social media co-promotion, 
visitor-guide listings, or other community announcements.
The item I am hoping to promote is:
[Event/business/program name]
[Date/time, if applicable]
[Location]
[Short description]
[Website or contact link]
Could you let me know which formats you accept, whether there are deadlines, and whether any options 
are free or paid?
Thank you,
[Name]
[Organization]
[Phone/email]
11. Required QA Checks Before Publishing
 Every row has a source URL.
 Every row has a verification status tag.
 No row claims paid advertising is available unless source explicitly confirms it.
 No row claims free placement unless source explicitly confirms it.
 No row claims guaranteed publication or guaranteed event-calendar inclusion.
 All uncertain details are marked Unknown - verify or Needs contact confirmation.
 The public language says ask about advertising, request listing, submit event, or verify placement - not put your ads in these.
 Mobile view preserves source and verification information, even if the table collapses into cards.
 The methodology section explains how channels were found and classified.
 The correction/intake form lets users submit new amplifier channels or report changed information.
12. Minimal Agent Execution Order
1. Insert Regional Amplifier Channels section after Start Here.
2. Add category cards/tabs.
3. Add intro and disclaimer copy.
4. Add master channel table using required schema.
5. Populate only verified rows from this brief.
6. Add source URLs and verification tags.
7. Add promotion packet checklist.
8. Add outreach template.
9. Link to relevant templates and forms.
10. Run QA checks for unsupported claims.
11. Mark remaining uncertain items as Needs verification.
12. Publish only after checking all links.
13. Known Follow-Up Work
 Verify exact submission deadlines for each channel.
 Verify whether each channel accepts paid ads, free announcements, editorial mentions, or only internal listings.
 Verify current contact person or department for each submission path.
 Find additional individual-business newsletters from businesses listed on the landing site.
 Repeat keyword searches quarterly: subscribe, newsletter, sign up, mailing list, events, calendar, submit event, business 
directory, get listed, visitor guide, advertising, media kit.
 Add confirmed channel rows only after source URL, proof type, and status tag are recorded.
Appendix: Methodology Language for the Site
This section identifies regional amplifier channels found through source-page review. A channel is 
listed when public page content shows evidence of a newsletter/signup system, event calendar, event 
submission pathway, business directory, business listing intake, visitor guide, social media co-op, 
venue lineup, or municipal update system. Advertising availability, deadlines, pricing, and acceptance 
rules are not assumed. Users should verify those details directly with each organization.
End of brief
